info.title: XmlDocMdSymbols
info.description: A .NET tool that generates Markdown from .NET XML documentation comments.

x-inspectra.cliParsedDescription: Generates Markdown from .NET XML documentation comments.

options.name: --source
options.description:
The URL (absolute or relative) of the folder containing the source
code of the assembly, e.g. at GitHub. (optional)

options.name: --namespace
options.description: The root namespace of the input assembly. (optional)

options.name: --visibility
options.description: The minimum visibility of documented members. (default 'protected')

options.name: --obsolete
options.description: Generates documentation for obsolete types and members.

options.name: --skip-unbrowsable
options.description: Skips documentation for types that are marked with System.ComponentModel.EditorBrowsable Never.

options.name: --skip-compiler-generated
options.description: Skips documentation for types that are marked with System.Runtime.CompilerServices.CompilerGenerated.

options.name: --clean
options.description: Deletes previously generated files that are no longer used.

options.name: --verify
options.description: Exits with error code 1 if changes to the file system are needed.

options.name: --dryrun
options.description: Executes the tool without making changes to the file system.

options.name: --front-matter
options.description:
File containing the Jekyll front matter template you want in each generated page.
The front matter can use $title argument and $rel for permalinks.
When front matter is defined the .md extension is dropped in all generated links.

options.name: --permalink
options.description:
Specify permalink style, 'none' or 'pretty' (default 'none').
'pretty' permalinks do not contain file extensions, and when you select this option.
periods have to be removed from file names, for example, 'System.Console' would have to be 'SystemConsole'.
since the removal of the '.md' extension would make Jekyll think .Console is a file extension which doesn't work.

options.name: --namespace-pages
options.description: Generate separate pages for each namespace, listing types in each.

options.name: --toc
options.description: File containing table of contents in .yml format.

options.name: --newline
options.description: The newline used in the output (default auto).
